items.title: upload page ownership
items.content: the owners are the product, platform, and design teams. this is not yet locked.

items.title: document versioning strategy
items.content: all uploaded documents must be versioned from day one. uploading a file with an existing name will create a new version and will not overwrite the old one.

items.title: user document upload workflow
items.content: 1. user lands on the upload page. 2. clicks 'upload docs' cta, which opens a file picker. 3. user selects files (.md,.pdf,.txt). 4. clicks 'start ingest' to begin processing. 5. a row appears in the table with a 'processing' status. 6. when complete, the status changes to 'extracted'. 7. user can then view the extraction.

items.title: supported file formats
items.content: for the initial release, only.md,.pdf, and.txt file formats will be supported for upload.

items.title: upload input validation and error handling
items.content: uploads must be rejected if the file is empty or has an unsupported extension. to prevent duplicate submissions, the 'start ingest' button should be disabled while a request is in-flight.

items.title: network interruption during upload
items.content: a network drop during upload is a known failure mode. the system should provide the user with an option to retry.

items.title: persisted document metadata
items.content: the core data points to persist for each uploaded document are: document_id, document_version, original_file_name, source_type (initially 'manual_upload'), and upload_status.

items.title: service dependencies
items.content: the upload page depends on the upload service api, the extraction job queue, and the document metadata store.

items.title: asynchronous extraction process
items.content: document extraction is an asynchronous process that begins immediately after a successful upload. the status of the job must persist, allowing the user to navigate away from the page and return later.

items.title: define batch upload limits
items.content: the maximum number of files allowed in a single batch upload needs to be determined. suggestions range from 20 to 50, depending on the async queue's health.

items.title: resolve open questions on upload behavior and features
items.content: decisions are needed on several topics: whether failed uploads should auto-retry, support for zip archives, timing for document-level tagging, and whether drag-and-drop initiates an instant upload or requires confirmation.

items.title: plan for future ingestion sources
items.content: future work includes building integrations to ingest documents directly from sources like notion and google drive.

items.title: challenge to 'never overwrite' rule
items.content: the decision to never overwrite files is being challenged for specific use cases like replacing a file to fix a typo. this rule may need to be revisited.

items.title: finalize upload status definitions
items.content: the official set of upload status values needs to be finalized with the backend team. the current set is `processing|extracted|failed`, with `queued` as a potential addition.

items.title: generate knowledge diff workflow
items.content: 1. user clicks `compare versions` on a document page. 2. a modal opens with dropdowns for old and new versions. 3. user selects two versions. 4. user clicks `generate knowledge diff`. 5. the system analyzes changes and displays added, removed, and modified knowledge sections. 6. user can expand modified cards to see old vs. new content.

items.title: default comparison versions
items.content: the default comparison view should be the latest version versus the immediately preceding version.

items.title: handling of wording-only changes
items.content: changes that are only wording adjustments should not be shown as modified knowledge. only semantic changes (e.g., changes to threshold values) must be shown.

items.title: read-only comparison view
items.content: the compare versions screen must be read-only; users cannot edit content from this interface.

items.title: handling for single version documents
items.content: if only one version of a document exists, the compare feature should be disabled, accompanied by a tooltip explaining why.

items.title: display logic for change reasons
items.content: modified item cards should include a 'why changed' reason if the analysis model provides one. if the reason is missing, the ui should display 'reason not provided'.

items.title: error handling for same version comparison
items.content: if a user attempts to compare the same version against itself, the system should show a warning and stop the process.

items.title: cost of auto-run compare feature
items.content: product wants the comparison to auto-run for speed, but the infrastructure team has raised concerns that this approach could lead to high operational costs. a manual run is preferred to control costs.

items.title: user confusion with non-adjacent version comparison
items.content: the design team has raised a concern that allowing users to compare non-adjacent versions (e.g., v1 vs. v5) may be confusing for users.

items.title: incomplete comparison due to failed extractions
items.content: if an extraction process failed for an older version, the system cannot perform a diff against a newer, successful extraction.

items.title: model timeout during analysis
items.content: the analysis model may time out during the diff generation process, which will interrupt the user workflow.

items.title: resolve auto-run vs. manual-run conflict
items.content: a decision needs to be made between product's request for auto-running comparisons for speed and infrastructure's concern for cost control via manual runs.

items.title: decide on 'strict diff' mode
items.content: legal has requested an optional 'strict diff' mode that would show all wording-only changes, which conflicts with the current decision to hide them. this requirement needs to be resolved.

items.title: add warning for missing extraction data
items.content: an explicit warning needs to be added to the ui for cases where a comparison cannot be run because the extraction data for an old version is missing.

items.title: evaluate including 'link changes' in diff
items.content: a determination needs to be made (tbd) on whether the comparison logic should include changes to links, not just knowledge items.

items.title: decide on report download feature
items.content: an open question exists on whether to allow users to download a markdown report of the comparison results.
